title: Listen and Choose a Response — Campus Services

questions.stem: Can I make an appointment at the health center for a flu shot?

options.A: Yes, you can book online or call the front desk.
options.B: Flu shots are recommended every year.
options.C: The health center is on the west side of campus.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking how to access a health service, so providing booking options directly answers their question.

questions.stem: The light in my dorm room keeps flickering. Can someone fix it?

options.A: I'll submit a maintenance request for you right now.
options.B: Flickering lights are usually a wiring issue.
options.C: You should buy a new bulb.

questions.explanation: The speaker is reporting a maintenance problem, so offering to take action and submit a request is the appropriate staff response.

questions.stem: Are there any work-study positions still available in the admissions office?

options.A: We have one opening for a front desk assistant. Would you like to apply?
options.B: Work-study jobs are popular among students.
options.C: The admissions office is very busy in the fall.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about job availability, so providing specific information about an opening and next steps is directly helpful.

questions.stem: Would it be possible to get a two-day extension on the lab report? I've been sick.

options.A: That's fine. Please submit it by Friday and attach a brief note.
options.B: Extensions are rarely granted in this department.
options.C: You should take better care of your health.

questions.explanation: The speaker is politely requesting an extension with a valid reason, so a compassionate approval with clear conditions is appropriate.

questions.stem: When does the debate club usually meet? I'm interested in joining.

options.A: We meet every Wednesday at 6 PM in the student union.
options.B: Debate clubs help with public speaking skills.
options.C: Many students join clubs in their first year.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about meeting times because they want to join, so providing the specific schedule is the most useful response.

questions.stem: The writing center tutors were so helpful with my thesis. I wanted to share that feedback.

options.A: Thank you for letting us know. I'll pass that along to the tutoring staff.
options.B: The writing center was renovated last year.

questions.explanation: The speaker is giving positive feedback about a service, so thanking them and confirming the feedback will be shared is professional.

questions.stem: How long does it take to get an official transcript mailed to my home address?

options.A: Official transcripts are mailed within 3 to 5 business days.
options.B: Transcripts include all courses you've taken.
options.C: You can also request an unofficial copy online.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a processing timeline, so providing the specific timeframe directly answers their question.

questions.stem: I'd rather work on the presentation than the research paper. Is that okay?

options.A: Sure, we can divide the tasks that way. What section will you present?
options.B: Research papers are more important than presentations.
options.C: I prefer the research paper myself.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing a preference for task division, so accommodating the preference and clarifying next steps is cooperative.

audioSegments.text: Can I make an appointment at the health center for a flu shot?
The light in my dorm room keeps flickering. Can someone fix it?
Are there any work-study positions still available in the admissions office?
Would it be possible to get a two-day extension on the lab report? I've been sick.
When does the debate club usually meet? I'm interested in joining.
The writing center tutors were so helpful with my thesis. I wanted to share that feedback.
How long does it take to get an official transcript mailed to my home address?
I'd rather work on the presentation than the research paper. Is that okay?
